title: The Peterhead-Goldeneye Gas Post-combustion CCS Project
authors: Bill Spence; Denise Horan; Owain Tucker
why_relevant: Public Goldeneye CCS project paper; useful for pipeline, storage, transport distance, capture rate and project-context assumptions.
next_action: Extract model-relevant facts into a notes file with page/section traceability.

title: A technical evaluation to analyse of potential repurposing of submarine pipelines for hydrogen and CCS using survival analysis
authors: Ramy Magdy A. Mahmoud; Paul E. Dodds
why_relevant: Directly relevant to submarine pipeline repurposing for CCS and hydrogen; uses survival analysis and includes Goldeneye as a CO2 case study.
next_action: Use as a benchmark source for remaining-life screening and compare its Goldeneye assumptions with our model.

title: Repurposing natural gas pipelines for hydrogen: Limits and options from a case study in Germany
authors: Kornel Telessy; Lukas Barner; Franziska Holz
why_relevant: Hydrogen-focused but useful for defining repurposing scope options and cost comparison logic: enhanced maintenance, gaseous inhibitors, and pipe-in-pipe.
next_action: Use as a supporting source for the repurposing-scope and cost-structure design, not as a direct CO2 integrity standard.

title: Assessing Compatibility of Natural Gas Pipeline Materials with Hydrogen, CO2, and Ammonia
authors: Michael D. Kass; James R. Keiser; Yan Liu; Amy Moore; Yarom Polsky
publication: Journal of Pipeline Systems Engineering and Practice 14(2):04023007
why_relevant: Directly useful for material compatibility screening of natural gas pipeline components with gaseous CO2, supercritical CO2, hydrogen blends and ammonia.
next_action: Use to build a material/component compatibility warning matrix for the repurposing gate, especially for supercritical CO2 compressor/regulator and polymer concerns.

title: Assessing the Feasibility of Repurposing the Existing Natural Gas Pipelines for Hydrogen Transport - A Comprehensive Review
why_relevant: Hydrogen-focused review; useful for case-by-case screening logic, pressure reduction, steel grade, welds, leakage, monitoring and pressure cycling cautions.
next_action: Use as supporting evidence for hydrogen-related warnings and general repurposing screening discipline, not as a direct CO2 model source.

title: Strategic Repurposing of Existing Oil Pipeline for CO2 gas-phase Transport: A Feasibility Framework
publication: Proceedings of the 2026 Pipeline Technology Conference
why_relevant: Highly relevant DNV conference paper on feasibility gates for repurposing existing oil and gas pipelines to gas-phase CO2 service.
next_action: Use to formalise the repurposing gate: initiation, screening, validation, execution, operating envelope, CO2 specification, safety, integrity, modification scope and LCOT.
